blocks:
Ayahs

إِذَا ٱلشَّمْسُ كُوِّرَتْ (۱)
When the sun is wound up, (1)
وَإِذَا ٱلنُّجُومُ ٱنكَدَرَتْ (۲)
when the stars scatter, (2)
وَإِذَا ٱلْجِبَالُ سُيِّرَتْ (۳)
when the mountains are set moving, (3)
وَإِذَا ٱلْعِشَارُ عُطِّلَتْ (٤)
when the pregnant camels are neglected, (4)
وَإِذَا ٱلْوُحُوشُ حُشِرَتْ (٥)
when the wild beasts are mustered, (5)

Key Points

"Takwīr" (تكویر) means to wrap up or fold over, which, regarding the sun, leads to the gathering of its flames and its extinguishing. The movement of the mountains on the eve of the Resurrection results from a great earthquake that causes the mountains to collapse and disintegrate, and as mentioned in other verses, they flow like desert sand. ‘Ishār (عِشار) is the plural of ‘Ashrā’ (عَشْراء), meaning a ten-month pregnant camel, which was considered the most valuable possession among the Arabs. However, when the signs of the Resurrection appear, it will be abandoned, and no one will care for it. Wuhūsh (وحوش) is the plural of Wahsh (وحش), meaning an animal that never becomes tame with humans, such as predators. Some, citing the verse, «و ما من دابّة فى الارض و لا طائر یطیر بجناحیه الاّ امم امثالكم ما فرطنا فى الكتاب من شى ء ثمّ الى ربّهم یحشرون»(31), have said that the intent of this verse is also the gathering (resurrection) of animals on the Day of Judgment. However, other commentators, noting that the context of the verses relates to the signs preceding the Resurrection, have said: the intention is that before the Resurrection, due to successive earthquakes, all animals will leave their dens and thickets and gather together.(32)

31) An‘ām, 38. 32) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The existing system of the sun and stars in the sky, and the mountains, sea, and earth, will one day be transformed. «اذا الشمس كوّرت و...»

2- Existence is subject to the Will of Allah, and whenever He wills, the order of the universe will be disrupted. «اذا الشمس كوّرت و...»

3- Perhaps wild animals will also be resurrected on the Day of Judgment. «و اذا الوحوش حُشرت»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah At-Takwīr

This Surah has twenty-nine verses and was revealed in Mecca. The name of the Surah is taken from the first verse and means the folding up and darkening. The content of the Surah indicates that it was revealed in the early days of the Prophethood, because the opponents, in the beginning of the call, used to call the Prophet mad, and this Surah, like Surah Al-Qalam which was revealed early in the Prophethood, contains the purification (exoneration) of the Prophet from such accusations.(29) The first part of this Surah describes the massive and comprehensive changes of this world that precede the establishment of the Resurrection, and the second part explains the high status of the Quran and its role and effect on the souls of human beings. Many virtues have been narrated regarding the recitation of this Surah, which serves as a reminder and removes heedlessness from man.(30)

29) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 30) Tafsir Nur Ath-Thaqalayn.

Ayahs

وَإِذَا ٱلْبِحَارُ سُجِّرَتْ (٦)
when the seas are set afire, (6)
وَإِذَا ٱلنُّفُوسُ زُوِّجَتْ (۷)
when the souls are assorted, (7)
وَإِذَا ٱلْمَوْءُۥدَةُ سُئِلَتْ (۸)
when the girl buried-alive will be asked (8)
بِأَىِّ ذَنۢبٍ قُتِلَتْ (۹)
for what sin she was killed. (9)
وَإِذَا ٱلصُّحُفُ نُشِرَتْ (۱٠)
When the records [of deeds] are unfolded, (10)
وَإِذَا ٱلسَّمَآءُ كُشِطَتْ (۱۱)
when the sky is stripped off, (11)
وَإِذَا ٱلْجَحِيمُ سُعِّرَتْ (۱۲)
when hell is set ablaze, (12)
وَإِذَا ٱلْجَنَّةُ أُزْلِفَتْ (۱۳)
when paradise is brought near, (13)
عَلِمَتْ نَفْسٌ مَّآ أَحْضَرَتْ (۱٤)
then a soul shall know what it has readied [for itself]. (14)

Key Points

The word «سُجّرت» has been given several meanings: 1. Boiling up 2. Joining together 3. The seas being filled and overflowing. All of these are natural, because on the one hand, the mountains will be uprooted and the seas will connect to one another, and on the other hand, severe earthquakes will spill out the molten material from inside the earth, causing the waters to boil and turn into steam.

In the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah), the Houris of Paradise will be married to the fortunate inhabitants of Paradise. As He says in other verses: «و زوّجناهم بحور عین» (33)

And the inhabitants of Hell will be married to their satanic companions. As He says in another verse: «و من یعش عن ذكر الرّحمن نقیّض له شیطاناً فهو له قرین» (34)

As Imam Baqir عليه السلام said in the interpretation of the verse «و اذا النفوس زوِّجت»: The people of faith will be paired with their heavenly spouses, and the people of Hell will be paired with Satan. (35)

«موؤدة» means a girl buried alive. One of the very bad customs of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah) was that sometimes, due to poverty or a sense of shame, they would bury their daughters alive.

The guardianship (wilayah) of parents over their child is limited, and they do not have the right to take away the natural rights of the child, such as the right to life. A father cannot say, "This is my child, and I have the right to make any decision regarding him," just as a mother cannot say, "I did not want to have a child, and I have the right to abort the fetus in my womb."

33) Ad-Dukhan, 54. 34) Az-Zukhruf, 36. 35) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Defending the oppressed is not limited by Islam or disbelief (kufr). Although the girls buried alive were not Muslims, the Quran defends their right. «و اذا الموؤدة سئلت»

2- Defending the oppressed is not limited by gender, age (small or large), or number (individual or group); even a small girl must be defended. «باىّ ذنب قتلت»

3- Awaken consciences through questioning. «بأىّ ذنب قتلت»

4- The guardianship of a father over his child does not include the right to kill the child. «بأىّ ذنب قتلت»

5- Fear and hope side by side are constructive. «الجحیم سُعِّرت - الجنّة اُزلفت»

6- On the Day of Judgment, Paradise and Hell will be brought near to their inhabitants. «الجنّة ازلفت» (And in Ayah 23 of Surah Al-Fajr, He says: «و جى ء یومئذ بجهنّم» — on that Day, Hell will be brought forth.)

7- Human deeds are recorded in this world, and the record of everyone's deeds will be opened on the Day of Judgment. «و اذا الصحف نشرت»

Ayahs

فَلَآ أُقْسِمُ بِٱلْخُنَّسِ (۱٥)
So I swear by the stars that return, (15)
ٱلْجَوَارِ ٱلْكُنَّسِ (۱٦)
the comets, (16)
وَٱلَّيْلِ إِذَا عَسْعَسَ (۱۷)
by the night as it approaches, (17)
وَٱلصُّبْحِ إِذَا تَنَفَّسَ (۱۸)
by the dawn as it breathes: (18)
إِنَّهُۥ لَقَوْلُ رَسُولٍ كَرِيمٍ (۱۹)
it is indeed the speech of a noble apostle, (19)
ذِى قُوَّةٍ عِندَ ذِى ٱلْعَرْشِ مَكِينٍ (۲٠)
powerful and eminent with the Lord of the Throne, (20)
مُّطَاعٍ ثَمَّ أَمِينٍ (۲۱)
one who is obeyed and is trustworthy as well. (21)

Key Points

Khunnas (خُنَّس) is the plural of khanis (خانس), meaning "that which returns" or "that which recedes." Jawar (جَوار) is the plural of jariya (جاریة), meaning "that which flows" or "that which is in motion," and kunnas (كُنَّس) is the plural of kanis (كانس), meaning "that which hides." Commentators, considering the subsequent verses which concern the end of the night and the breaking of dawn, have interpreted the verses "فلا اقسم بالخنّس الجوار الكنّس" (So I swear by the receding stars, the moving, the hiding) as referring to the stars, which appear every night and hide with the rising of the sun, although even while hidden, they are in motion, and thus return to their place the following night. ‘As‘as (عَسعس) means a thin darkness, which, according to the statement of Imam Ali عليه السلام, is observed at the end of the night.(36) Therefore, the verse "و الّیل اذا عسعس" (And the night when it draws to a close) is similar to the verse "والّیل اذ ادبر" (And the night when it departs).(37) Tanaffas (تَنَفَّس) means the breathing of the morning. It is as if the day has been freed from the confinement of the night and breathes in the open air. Allah’s swearing by the breaking of dawn, the hiding of the stars, and the movement of celestial bodies, at a time when people were living in the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), is a sign of Islam's attention to nature and reflection upon it.

36) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan. 37) Al-Muddaththir, 33.

Messages

1- The Day of Judgment is the day of witnessing and the embodiment of deeds. "علمت نفس ما احضرت" 2- On the Day of Judgment, man becomes aware of the details of his deeds. "ما احضرت" 3- The Angel of Revelation (Gabriel) is noble, trustworthy, strong, and obeyed. "كریم، ذى قوّة مكین، مطاع، امین" 4- Nobility and honor, capability and secure position, having obedient forces, and trustworthiness are necessary conditions for conveying the message and guidance. "كریم، ذى قوّة، مكین، مطاع، امین" 5- Gabriel has obedient agents at his disposal. "مطاع"

Ayahs

وَمَا صَاحِبُكُم بِمَجْنُونٍ (۲۲)
Your companion is not crazy: (22)
وَلَقَدْ رَءَاهُ بِٱلْأُفُقِ ٱلْمُبِينِ (۲۳)
certainly he saw him on the manifest horizon, (23)
وَمَا هُوَ عَلَى ٱلْغَيْبِ بِضَنِينٍ (۲٤)
and he is not miserly concerning the Unseen. (24)
وَمَا هُوَ بِقَوْلِ شَيْطَـٰنٍ رَّجِيمٍ (۲٥)
And it is not the speech of an outcast Satan. (25)

Key Points

Ḍanīn (ضَنین) is derived from ḍann (ضَنّ), meaning to be stingy or avaricious. This word has been used only once in the Quran. The opponents of the Prophet attributed madness (majnūn) to him, and their intention was that the Prophet was connected to the jinn. In reality, they considered the Quran to be the inspirations (or suggestions) of the jinn upon the Prophet. Therefore, these verses, in refuting this accusation, state: The Prophet clearly saw the Angel of Revelation and received the Quran from him, and it is not the case that Satan cast something upon the Prophet's heart and he mistakenly thought it was Divine revelation.

Messages

1- Sometimes accusations must not be left unanswered; they must be responded to. «و ما صاحبكم بمجنون»

2- The Prophet lived alongside the people and among them. «صاحبكم»

3- The best sign of the Prophet's sound intellect is that you have associated and interacted with him for a lifetime and have never witnessed any improper thought, deed, speech, or behavior from him. «و ما صاحبكم بمجنون»

4- The Prophet saw Gabriel. «و لقد رآه» (And indeed he saw him) (Just as Surah An-Najm, Ayah 13 states: «لقد رآه نزلةً اخرى»)

5- In response to insults, honoring those who were insulted is necessary. «لقد رآه بالافق المبین»

6- There is no ambiguity regarding the location of the Prophet's meeting with Gabriel. «الافق المبین»

7- The gratitude for knowledge is its dissemination, and academic stinginess is disliked. «و ما هو على الغیب بضنین»

8- The Noble Prophet possessed knowledge of the unseen. «و ما هو على الغیب بضنین»

9- The Messenger of Allah is neither afflicted by madness internally: «ما صاحبكم بمجنون» (Your companion is not mad), nor is he influenced by satanic suggestions externally. «و ما هو بقول شیطان رجیم»

10- The Quran is protected from any distortion during the stage of revelation, and Gabriel makes no interference or alteration in it, «رسول كریم... امین» (A noble Messenger... trustworthy), just as it suffers no deficiency or omission during the stage of transmission. «و ما هو على الغیب بضنین»

11- The Quran is the speech of the Angel of Revelation, not the rejected Satan. «لقول رسول كریم... ما هو بقول شیطان رجیم»

Ayahs

فَأَيْنَ تَذْهَبُونَ (۲٦)
So where are you going? (26)
إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ لِّلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۲۷)
It is just a reminder for all the nations, (27)
لِمَن شَآءَ مِنكُمْ أَن يَسْتَقِيمَ (۲۸)
for those of you who wish to walk straight; (28)
وَمَا تَشَآءُونَ إِلَّآ أَن يَشَآءَ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۲۹)
but you will not wish unless it is wished by Allah, the Lord of all the worlds. (29)

Messages

1- We do not have aimless or unprincipled humans; even disbelievers and polytheists have a path and doctrine for themselves. (لكم دینكم) What is important is choosing the correct path. (این تذهبون)

2- The Quran is the best means of counsel, reminder, and admonition. (ان هو الاّ ذكر)

3- The Quran is for all the worlds (mankind) and is not restricted to a specific time, place, or race. (للعالمین)

4- In the method of propagation, reminding is yes, but compulsion is no. (ذكر للعالمین لمن شاء منكم ان یستقیم)

5- Finding the straight path and remaining steadfast upon it depends on the will of the human being himself. (لمن شاء منكم ان یستقیم)

6- The path of the Quran is the straight path, free from any form of extremism, negligence, or deviation. (لمن شاء منكم ان یستقیم)

7- Our will is within the orbit of Allah’s Will, not by His compulsion, for in that case, our will would become meaningless. (و ما تشاؤن الاّ ان یشاء اللّه)

8- Man is neither without will nor completely autonomous. (و ما تشاؤن الاّ ان یشاء اللّه)

9- The Will (Mashi’ah) of Allah is based on His Lordship (Rububiyyah). (یشاء اللّه ربّ العالمین)

10- Since Allah is the Lord of the Worlds (Rabb al-'Alamin) and has dominance over all affairs, the will of man is also conditional upon His Will. (و ما تشاؤن الاّ ان یشاء اللّه ربّ العالمین)

For example, in driving instruction cars, there are two sets of gas and brake pedals: one under the trainee's feet and the other under the instructor's feet. In this car, the trainee can use the gas and brake on his side, but only on the condition that the instructor also wills it. Or, similar to dual-signature checks, where each of the two signatories can sign, but the effect and payment of the check only occur when the other person has also signed it.
